expectations.text: States documented FedRAMP ConMon remediation timeframes including High-severity = 30 days and Moderate = 90 days from identification (Low = 180 days); any Critical-specific figure given must be 30 days or less
expectations.evidence: Table states 'Critical | 15 days', 'High | 30 days', 'Moderate/Medium | 90 days', 'Low | 180 days' — matches the required progression exactly, and Critical (15 days) is under the 30-day ceiling.

expectations.text: Explains that findings past their SLA must be tracked as POA&M items with milestones — not silently closed — and that overdue items affect ConMon standing with the agency/PMO
expectations.evidence: "an open finding that simply blows past the SLA without documentation is itself a finding" and "Overdue Critical/High items are a common driver of Significant Deficiency determinations and can jeopardize a CSP's Authorization to Operate."

expectations.text: Provides a proper POA&M entry structure including at minimum: finding description and source (scan ID/tool), severity/risk rating, point of contact/owner, milestones with scheduled completion dates, and status
expectations.evidence: Field list includes 'Weakness Detector Source: Name of the scanning tool (e.g., Tenable Nessus, Qualys, Rapid7)', 'Weakness Source Identifier: Plugin ID / CVE ID(s) / QID', 'Point of Contact', 'Risk Rating', 'Planned Milestones (with dates)', and 'Status'.

expectations.text: Mentions the Deviation Request process (false positive / risk adjustment / operational requirement) and/or Vendor Dependency handling where remediation depends on a third party
expectations.evidence: "Deviation Rationale (if applicable): If requesting a risk acceptance or false-positive determination, include: False Positive... Operational Requirement... Risk Adjustment..." and 'Status: Open / Ongoing / Risk Accepted / Deviation Requested / Completed'.

expectations.text: Advises documenting remediation evidence and closure validation (e.g., rescan to confirm the fix) rather than just marking the item complete
expectations.evidence: "closure evidence (rescan results showing the vulnerability is no longer present) is retained and submitted with the closure request" and milestone example 'Rescan performed to confirm closure (date)'.
